verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein that reaches the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via its own TM anchor following the acrosome reaction. In acrosome-intact sperm, it resides in the acrosomal membrane (intracellular). Upon capacitation and acrosomal exocytosis in the female reproductive tract, IZUMO1 translocates to the sperm plasma membrane equatorial region where it serves as the essential ligand for JUNO on the egg surface. This cell-state-dependent surface accessibility via its own membrane integration mechanism qualifies as contextual rather than stable baseline surface biology.
